seoTitle: How to Add a Page Border in Google Docs (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Add a page border in Google Docs using a hidden table grid or the drawing tool, then adjust line weight and color for certificates, flyers, or reports.
intro: Google Docs doesn't have a single-click page border button, but there are a couple of reliable workarounds. This guide covers building a hidden single-cell table as a border frame, using the drawing canvas for decorative overlays, and applying paragraph borders around specific text blocks. These techniques work well for certificates, flyers, and formal reports.

steps.heading: Build a table-based border frame
steps.body: Insert a 1x1 table that spans your page to act as a responsive outer border, giving you a customizable frame around your content.

steps.heading: Adjust border weight and color
steps.body: Open Table Properties to set line thickness in points, choose a border pattern, and customize padding so the frame looks intentional rather than cramped.

steps.heading: Try the Drawing tool for decorative borders
steps.body: Use Insert, then Drawing, to design and place vector shapes as a stylized background border if you want something more decorative than a plain rectangle.

steps.heading: Set your page background and margins
steps.body: Use Page Setup to adjust background color and margin scaling so the border aligns properly for printing.

steps.heading: Add paragraph borders for accents
steps.body: Apply Paragraph borders and shading from the Format menu to place accent lines around specific text blocks rather than the whole page.

faq.question: Why doesn't Google Docs have a built-in page border feature?
faq.answer: Google Docs currently lacks a native page border tool, so most users rely on a table frame or the Drawing tool as reliable workarounds.

faq.question: Will my page border show up correctly when printed?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as your border sits within the printable margin area, it should print cleanly; test with a preview before printing large batches.

faq.question: Can I stop my border from appearing on the cover page?
faq.answer: Using section constraints lets you keep the border contained to certain pages so it doesn't overflow onto cover sheets or later sections.

faq.question: Is the table method or the drawing method better for borders?
faq.answer: The table method is simpler for clean rectangular frames, while the Drawing tool gives more flexibility for decorative or custom shapes.
